_note: Layer name format: <Role>-<Discipline>-<Number>-<Type>. Role indicates who produces it; Discipline + Number identify the element; Type indicates the graphical representation.
_taxonomy_scope: BS 1192:2007+A2:2016 §6.3 layer-naming codes — aligns with ISO 13567 discipline subset (14 codes for the layer-naming context). For BS 1192 §7 file-naming role codes (22 codes, broader scope), see project-codes.json.
format_example: M-E-LITE-EM (Model layer, Electrical discipline, Lighting elements, Emergency-power modifier)

role._purpose: Indicates the layer's origin/purpose within the CDE

codes.M: Model — graphical elements forming the building/asset model
codes.D: Drafting — annotation and detailing not in the model

element_number._note: Element codes are discipline-specific. For Electrical discipline, see cad-layers.json layout_group→CAD-layer-code mapping which gives the canonical Electrical element set (POWR, LITE, COMM, FIRE, etc.)

type_modifier._purpose: Sub-type or modifier indicating graphical representation or status
